purpose: Evidence that the starter-independent V2.2 matrix builds reproducibly from team + IANA context (park + DST-aware schedule/travel) only, carries no starter-dependent or 2025 column, and shares the V2 target/exclusion logic.

cross_consistency_with_v2.note: V2.2 shares the identical target-label and row-exclusion logic with NRFI_CORE_V2, so the following must and do match V2 exactly.

starter_independence.guard: assert_admissible_columns() runs over the full sorted column set and fails the build closed on any hit; verified 0 hits across all 239 columns.

missingness_vs_schema_drift.policy: Every continuous and binary feature carries an explicit <col>_missing indicator (116 indicators). Categoricals are fixed-vocabulary one-hot; a null category sets <name>_missing, while a non-null UNKNOWN category raises CoreV22MatrixError (schema drift) - the two are never conflated.

primary_eligibility_rule: primary_feature_eligible = away_team_context_eligible AND home_team_context_eligible AND park_context_eligible AND away_schedule_travel_eligible AND home_schedule_travel_eligible (NO pitcher, NO workload). The model and the expanding-climatology baseline are scored on exactly these identical primary rows.

import_hygiene_change.change: pandas / pyarrow moved from module-top imports to lazy imports inside the three functions that use them (_write_parquet, _numeric, build_pitcher_game_history); added a TYPE_CHECKING-only pandas import for the pd.DataFrame annotation.
import_hygiene_change.why: team_features borrows only pure json/hash helpers (_identity, _ratio, _write_json, _write_jsonl, canonical_json_bytes) from pitcher_statcast; the top-level pyarrow import forced the entire starter-independent path to load the parquet stack. The change is import-mechanics only.
import_hygiene_change.identity_preservation: No emitted artifact changes. Verified locally that team_features still reproduces features_identity 5124bebb; CI re-verifies pitcher features and the frozen V2 matrix identity 83003ad4 are byte-unchanged.

tests.coverage: forbidden-column guard (substrings + prefix patterns), continuous/binary/categorical emitters, null-missingness vs unknown-category schema-drift separation, fixed categorical vocab
